CheckTitle: Conditional Access policy enforces MFA for guest users
Description:
At least one Conditional Access policy requires **multifactor authentication** for all guest and external user types across all cloud applications.

This covers internal guests, B2B collaboration guests and members, B2B direct connect users, other external users, and service providers.
Risk: Guest and external users authenticate from environments outside the organization's control. Without MFA, a compromised guest credential can grant an attacker access to tenant resources, leading to **data exfiltration** or **lateral movement** across collaboration boundaries.

Code.Other:
1. Navigate to the Microsoft Entra admin center at https://entra.microsoft.com.
2. Expand **Protection** > **Conditional Access** and select **Policies**.
3. Click **New policy**.
4. Under **Users**, select **Include** > **Select users and groups** > check **Guest or external users** and select all guest types.
5. Under **Target resources**, include **All cloud apps**.
6. Under **Grant**, select **Grant Access** and check **Require multifactor authentication**.
7. Set the policy to **Report Only** for initial testing, then switch to **On**.
8. Click **Create**.

Recommendation.Text: Enforce MFA for all guest and external user types through a Conditional Access policy. Apply the **zero trust** principle to external identities by treating them as untrusted by default and requiring strong authentication before granting access to any cloud application.
